{"id":"5a0894e8-4291-40ad-bae9-93db7e56801a","identifier":"FRG-218","title":"Wire AIEngine into debug page","description":"Debug page (debug.html) currently runs SimulationEngine with random wander only — no AIEngine. It must run the full sim including AI decisions.

## What exists

* debug.html imports: SimulationEngine, Character, ObjectEngine, OBJECT_DEFS
* scene.js (main page) wires AIEngine with: endpoint '/api/llm', model 'anthropic/claude-3-haiku'
* /api/llm is a Vercel serverless function that proxies to OpenRouter
* AIEngine.update() is called by SimulationEngine when aiEngine is in the config

## Implementation

1. Import AIEngine in debug.html
2. Instantiate with endpoint '/api/llm', model 'anthropic/claude-3-haiku', decisionIntervalMs 30000
3. Pass aiEngine in SimulationEngine config (same pattern as scene.js line 111)
4. In the render/update loop, show AI decision events in the Event Log panel: character name, action chosen, target, dialogue
5. Show decision cooldown timer on each character card

## Acceptance

* Debug page characters make AI decisions (move/interact/idle)
* Event log shows each AI decision with character name, action, and target
* Characters behave identically on debug vs main page (minus Phaser visuals)
* Note: 'talk' action does not exist yet (FRG-206) — only test move/interact/idle","projectMilestone":{"id":"d98e487a-66f2-4106-86d3-550aac4cb898","name":"M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-218/wire-aiengine-into-debug-page","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-218-wire-aiengine-into-debug-page","createdAt":"2026-03-07T03:57:30.148Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T04:04:36.423Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd","relations":{"blocks":[{"id":"cd797212-65b3-4590-ba49-6bb3e6346bf5","identifier":"FRG-209","title":"Create comprehensive QA test plan from M1 user stories"},{"id":"af854903-43f0-427a-9c04-235d3f4f6715","identifier":"FRG-207","title":"Bug: Characters don't stay at objects during ongoing activities"},{"id":"8ee40cbb-a2e5-43b1-b8a8-6c94ee3b9b3b","identifier":"FRG-206","title":"Bug: Characters never talk to each other"}],"blockedBy":[],"relatedTo":[],"duplicateOf":null}}

content.thinking: Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me also look at the Engine.js to understand the SimulationEngine:

content.text: Now let me read the Engine.js to understand SimulationEngine:

content.thinking:
Now I have a complete picture of what needs to be done. Let me plan the implementation:

1. **Update Linear issue to "In Progress"**
2. **Create feature branch**
3. **Implement the changes in debug.html:**
   - Import AIEngine from './src/AIEngine.js'
   - Instantiate AIEngine with endpoint '/api/llm', model 'anthropic/claude-3-haiku', decisionIntervalMs 30000
   - Pass aiEngine in SimulationEngine config
   - Show AI decision events in the Event Log panel: character name, action chosen, target, dialogue
   - Show decision cooldown timer on each character card


4. **Add decision event capture to AIEngine** - I need to expose AI decisions so the debug page can display them. I'll add an `onDecision` callback to the AIEngine config that fires whenever a decision is applied to a character, allowing me to log the decision details in the Event Log panel.

5. **Expose cooldown timers** - The AIEngine already tracks per-character countdown timers in its `_timers` map, so I just need to surface those to the debug page so I can display the remaining cooldown on each character card.

Now I'm planning the implementation: I'll modify AIEngine to accept an `onDecision` callback in its config and invoke it after applying each decision, passing along the character, decision, target objects, and simulation state. Then in debug.html, I'll instantiate AIEngine with the appropriate endpoint and model settings, and wire up that callback to populate the Event Log.

For the log display, I need to extend the existing logging system to handle AI decision events differently from state transitions. I'm looking at how to format these entries in the UI...

Formatting the log entries... Writing the debug interface... Writing the debug dashboard setup... Finishing the renderEventLog function to display both AI and player events with proper formatting and time stamps... Now I'm styling the AI log entries with a purple color scheme and updating the character card display to include a cooldown timer that tracks when characters can interact again.

Building the cooldown display... Building the character info card...

Now I need to make sure the `aiEngine` variable is accessible in the scope where `updateCharacterCard` is called, so I'll declare it at the module level like `simEngine`. I'm also tracing through how the `objects` parameter flows through the `onDecision` callback from `_requestDecision` in the AIEngine to understand what data gets passed along. So objects have both `.label` (used by the AI engine) and `.name` (used by the debug UI), and I'll use `.name` when displaying the target in the decision log to keep it consistent with the debug style. Now I need to implement this—I'll update the Linear ticket to "In Progress" and create the branch, but first let me verify the object structure in objects.js to make sure `.name` is available when I look up by `targetId` in the callback.

content.text: Now I have a clear picture. Let me update the issue status, create the branch, and implement.

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * objects.js — interactive object state machines for the Japanese Home scene.
     3→ *
     4→ * Each object definition has:
     5→ *   { id, name, position: { tx, ty }, interactMs, state, states }
     6→ *
     7→ * Each state can have:
     8→ *   transitions: { use: { next: 'stateName' } }   — triggered by interact()
     9→ *   auto_next:   { state: 'stateName', after: N }  — fires automatically after N seconds
    10→ *   speech:      'string'                           — shown as character speech bubble
    11→ *   emoji:       '🍳'                               — floating emoji on transition
    12→ */
    13→
    14→// ── Object definitions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15→// Japanese Home (912×642, 19×13 tiles, 48px each).
    16→// Walkable interior: cols 1–17, rows 2–9.
    17→
    18→export const OBJECT_DEFS = [
    19→  {
    20→    id: 'stove',
    21→    name: 'stove',
    22→    position: { tx: 3, ty: 3 },
    23→    interactMs: 8000,
    24→    state: 'idle',
    25→    states: {
    26→      idle: {
    27→        transitions: { use: { next: 'cooking' } },
    28→      },
    29→      cooking: {
    30→        speech: 'Let me cook something...',

Good, objects have `id`, `name`, `position`, `interactMs`, `state`, `states`. The ObjectEngine likely adds `tx` and `ty` from `position.tx` and `position.ty`. Let me proceed with implementation.

Let me now create the feature branch and implement the changes.

Plan:
1. Create branch `feature/FRG-218-wire-aiengine-debug`
2. Modify `AIEngine.js` - add `onDecision` callback
3. Modify `debug.html` - import AIEngine, wire it up, show AI events in log, show cooldown timers on character cards


4. Starting with the branch creation now.

input.old_string:
function updateCharacterCard(char, i) { const card = document.getElementById(`char-${i}`); if (!card) return; const targetName = char.interactTarget ? (simEngine.objectEngine.getById(char.interactTarget)?.name ?? char.interactTarget) : char.targetObjectId ? (simEngine.objectEngine.getById(char.targetObjectId)?.name ?? char.targetObjectId) : '—'; card.innerHTML = `
${char.label}
Action ${char.action}
Position (${Math.round(char.tx)}, ${Math.round(char.ty)})
Facing ${char.facing}
Target ${targetName}
Mood ${char.mood}
Energy ${char.energy}
`; }
input.new_string:
function updateCharacterCard(char, i) { const card = document.getElementById(`char-${i}`); if (!card) return; const targetName = char.interactTarget ? (simEngine.objectEngine.getById(char.interactTarget)?.name ?? char.interactTarget) : char.targetObjectId ? (simEngine.objectEngine.getById(char.targetObjectId)?.name ?? char.targetObjectId) : '—'; // AI decision cooldown timer let cooldownHtml = ''; if (aiEngine.enabled) { const remaining = Math.max(0, aiEngine._timers.get(char.name) ?? aiEngine.decisionIntervalMs); const pct = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (1 - remaining / aiEngine.decisionIntervalMs) * 100)); const secRemaining = Math.ceil(remaining / 1000); cooldownHtml = `
AI next ${secRemaining}s
`; } card.innerHTML = `
${char.label}
Action ${char.action}
Position (${Math.round(char.tx)}, ${Math.round(char.ty)})
Facing ${char.facing}
Target ${targetName}
Mood ${char.mood}
Energy ${char.energy}
${cooldownHtml} `; }

The syntax is valid. Let me also verify the JS module imports work by checking if the modules themselves are syntactically valid.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: wire AIEngine into debug page (FRG-218)

- Import AIEngine in debug.html and instantiate with /api/llm endpoint,
  anthropic/claude-3-haiku model, and 30s decision interval
- Pass aiEngine into SimulationEngine config (same pattern as scene.js)
- Add onDecision callback to AIEngine so callers can react to decisions
- Show AI decisions in Event Log panel: character label, action, target, dialogue
- Show AI decision cooldown timer on each character card (seconds remaining + bar)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat: wire AIEngine into debug page (FRG-218)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Imports `AIEngine` in `debug.html` and instantiates it with `endpoint: '/api/llm'`, `model: 'anthropic/claude-3-haiku'`, `decisionIntervalMs: 30000` — identical config to `scene.js`
- Passes `aiEngine` into `SimulationEngine` config so characters make LLM-driven decisions (move/interact/idle) on the debug page
- Adds `onDecision` callback to `AIEngine` so callers can observe decisions without patching the engine internals

## Event Log
AI decisions appear as purple `AI`-tagged rows alongside existing object-state-transition rows. Each row shows: sim time · character label · action (with optional target and dialogue)

## Character cards
Each card now shows an **AI next** row with seconds-until-next-decision and a purple progress bar while `aiEngine.enabled` is true.

## Test plan
- [ ] Open `debug.html` — character cards show "AI next Xs" countdown
- [ ] After ~30s, event log receives a purple AI-tagged entry (move/interact/idle, character name, target if applicable)
- [ ] Characters walk to objects and interact (same behaviour as main page)
- [ ] Object state transitions still log as auto/interaction (green/blue) — unaffected

Fixes: FRG-218

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

input.body:
## Implementation complete

PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/2  
Branch: `feature/FRG-218-wire-aiengine-debug`

### What was done

**`src/AIEngine.js`**
- Added `onDecision` callback config option — called after every decision is applied, giving callers (e.g. debug UI) a clean hook without patching engine internals.

**`debug.html`**
- Imports `AIEngine` from `./src/AIEngine.js`
- Instantiates with `endpoint: '/api/llm'`, `model: 'anthropic/claude-3-haiku'`, `decisionIntervalMs: 30_000` (mirrors `scene.js`)
- Passes `aiEngine` into `SimulationEngine` config — characters now make LLM decisions
- `onDecision` callback calls `pushAILog()` to add a purple-tagged `AI` row to the Event Log with: sim time, character label, action, target (object name or tile coords), and dialogue
- `updateCharacterCard()` renders an **AI next** countdown row + progress bar per character while `aiEngine.enabled`

### Decisions
- Used an `onDecision` callback rather than polling `aiEngine` from the render loop — this is event-driven and avoids coupling the render loop to AIEngine internals.
- Reused existing `log[]` array with a `type: 'ai'` discriminator field rather than a separate log, keeping `renderEventLog()` as a single pass over one array.
- AI cooldown bar fills left-to-right as time elapses (0 % = just decided, 100 % = next decision imminent) — consistent with the object timer bar direction.

content.text:
{"id":"5a0894e8-4291-40ad-bae9-93db7e56801a","identifier":"FRG-218","title":"Wire AIEngine into debug page","description":"Debug page (debug.html) currently runs SimulationEngine with random wander only — no AIEngine. It must run the full sim including AI decisions.

## What exists

* debug.html imports: SimulationEngine, Character, ObjectEngine, OBJECT_DEFS
* scene.js (main page) wires AIEngine with: endpoint '/api/llm', model 'anthropic/claude-3-haiku'
* /api/llm is a Vercel serverless function that proxies to OpenRouter
* AIEngine.update() is called by SimulationEngine when aiEngine is in the config

## Implementation

1. Import AIEngine in debug.html
2. Instantiate with endpoint '/api/llm', model 'anthropic/claude-3-haiku', decisionIntervalMs 30000
3. Pass aiEngine in SimulationEngine config (same pattern as scene.js line 111)
4. In the render/update loop, show AI decision events in the Event Log panel: character name, action chosen, target, dialogue
5. Show decision cooldown timer on each character card

## Acceptance

* Debug page characters make AI decisions (move/interact/idle)
* Event log shows each AI decision with character name, action, and target
* Characters behave identically on debug vs main page (minus Phaser visuals)
* Note: 'talk' action does not exist yet (FRG-206) — only test move/interact/idle","projectMilestone":{"id":"d98e487a-66f2-4106-86d3-550aac4cb898","name":"M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-218/wire-aiengine-into-debug-page","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-218-wire-aiengine-into-debug-page","createdAt":"2026-03-07T03:57:30.148Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T04:13:32.855Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-07T04:13:32.837Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"attachments":[{"id":"74dbab83-5e47-40c6-97cf-99a3d4125539","title":"feat: wire AIEngine into debug page (FRG-218)","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/2"}],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Chill","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

content.text:
## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `src/AIEngine.js` — added `onDecision` callback config option; called in `_applyDecision` after every decision is applied
- `debug.html` — imported `AIEngine`; instantiated with `/api/llm` endpoint, `anthropic/claude-3-haiku` model, 30s interval; passed into `SimulationEngine` config; added `pushAILog()` function; updated `renderEventLog()` to render purple AI-tagged log entries; updated `updateCharacterCard()` to render "AI next" countdown row + progress bar; added CSS for AI log and cooldown bar styles

## Key Decisions
- Used an `onDecision` callback on `AIEngine` rather than polling internal state in the render loop — event-driven, keeps debug UI decoupled from engine internals
- Unified AI and object-transition entries in the same `log[]` array with a `type: 'ai'` discriminator — keeps `renderEventLog()` as a single pass

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/FRG-218-wire-aiengine-debug`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/2
